levers.how: compute directly on packed ternary; measured 4.13x vs fp32 paging (paged_lut.py)
route to ~1/8 experts per token -> 8x fewer active params (Day-15)
2-bit weights = ~8x fewer bytes to move; ~2x net after compute overlap
LUT table-build amortizes over big N (3.84x->higher on real expert sizes)

note: 101x speed = kernel x sparsity x bandwidth x matrix-size, vs NAIVE fp32 dense (not vs optimized llama.cpp). Compute-side; kernel+sparsity measured.
